commonRoofingChallenges: Military PCS timelines that compress real estate transactions and demand fast pre-sale roofing assessments
Cross-state insurance claim handling — adjusters work either side of the river but with different rules and timelines
Spring hail and wind events driving heavy claim volume
Illinois licensure requirement screens out unlicensed Missouri-only contractors
uniqueAngleElaboration: O'Fallon, Illinois sits adjacent to Scott Air Force Base, and that proximity shapes a meaningful share of our work in the city. PCS (Permanent Change of Station) cycles compress real estate timelines for military families — when a homeowner has 60 days to sell before reporting to a new base, a roof issue identified during the buyer's inspection can derail the entire sale. Fast pre-sale inspections, clear written reports, and rapid repair scheduling are essential. Beyond the military angle, O'Fallon IL is a fast-growing Metro East suburb with a housing mix that includes 1980s–2010s subdivision homes plus an older downtown core. Insurance claim work here follows Illinois rules — adjusting timelines, rebuild scope rules, and deductible structures can differ from Missouri-side claims. Roofing contractors working in O'Fallon need active Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation licensure, and homeowners should specifically verify Illinois licensure (separate from any Missouri credentials a contractor advertises).
